# OpenShield

**It can:** maintain a tamper-*evident* log with forward integrity.

**It cannot:** prevent a determined person from exfiltrating data · offer a
tamper-*proof* log (impossible in a single self-hosted trust domain) · guarantee
your data is safe against someone with root.

A deliberate marketing aside uses a term on purpose. <!-- allow: unhackable -->
Nobody calls this unhackable, but we discuss the word here.
